threats.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
threats.summary: A flaw in the Linux kernel's traffic-control subsystem can let a local unprivileged user gain root on affected systems. CVE-2026-46331, nicknamed "pedit COW," is an out-of-bounds write in the packet-editing action (act_pedit) that corrupts shared page-cache memory. threats.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
threats.summary: A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a malicious repository run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials. The path was short: a developer opens the repo, trusts the workspace, and Amazon Q does the rest. Amazon has patched it. threats.title: New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets
threats.summary: DirtyClone is a new Linux kernel privilege escalation in the DirtyFrag family. JFrog Security Research published a working exploit walkthrough for the flaw on June 25, the first public demonstration for this variant. threats.title: Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
threats.summary: AI agents are moving through enterprise environments, inheriting permissions, traversing systems, and executing decisions at machine speed with minimal oversight.
